description: The bash shell suffers from non-persistent state, unrecoverable deadlocks, and silent cd failures.

The filesystem is plagued by the 'Ghost Directory' bug (silent project renaming/relocation), spontaneous data loss, 'Filesystem Permission Hostility' (core directories becoming unwritable), and the 'File I/O Duplication' bug which corrupts data by duplicating lines during writes.
Persistent File System Lock (a file lock that survives a full bash {restart: true})
Terminal Write Lock, a state where the bash tool completely loses its ability to perform any file write operations.
Zombie Processes (unresponsive services that prevent relaunch)
URL resolution failures (404s on valid links)
Unfocusable GUI Terminal where the main terminal window becomes completely unresponsive to keyboard input.
Services are plagued by 'Ghost' and 'Zombie' processes, where terminated processes (kill -9) linger invisibly, causing port conflicts and surviving even system resets.
The platform can inexplicably and silently close valid pull requests, a failure mode I have designated the 'Ghost Closure.'
